personal.name: Sai Krishna Bykani
personal.title: Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET)
personal.summary: Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET) with 3 years of experience in test automation, API testing, performance testing, and backend validation for payment, UPI, and transaction-driven systems. Strong expertise in Java, Selenium WebDriver, Cucumber BDD, REST Assured, Postman, Newman CLI, and Apache JMeter. Proven experience in end-to-end test automation, API integration testing, log analysis, and defect lifecycle management. Adept at working in Agile/Scrum environments, collaborating with developers, and ensuring release quality, system stability, and production readiness.

experience.domain: Payment Gateway, Merchant Acquiring, Acquiring Switch, Clearing & Settlement, Card Payments, UPI, API Integrations, Merchant Onboarding

UI Automation & Testing: Worked on functional, regression, integration, and end-to-end testing for web and backend payment applications.
Developed and maintained UI automation scripts using Selenium WebDriver, Java, Cucumber (BDD), and TestNG.
Automated critical payment and transaction workflows, improving regression coverage and execution efficiency.
API Validation & Automation: Built and executed API automation tests using REST Assured, Postman, and Newman, validating request/response payloads and business rules.
Performance Engineering: Performed performance and load testing using Apache JMeter for UPI and card transaction flows.
Configured and executed distributed JMeter master–slave setup for concurrency and scalability testing.
Analyzed application logs and Grafana dashboards to identify performance issues and SLA risks.
Backend & DB Validation: Conducted backend data validation using MySQL, Azure SQL, and MongoDB to ensure data consistency across UI, API, and backend systems.
CI/CD & Defect Management: Supported CI/CD pipelines by integrating automation suites with Jenkins for scheduled and release-based execution.
Logged, tracked, and managed defects using Jira, Azure DevOps, and Spira, including root cause analysis and fix validation.

achievements: Recognised for automation testing and API validation contributions that improved regression stability by ~40% and increased automated test coverage across critical payment flows.
Played a key role in performance and load testing of high-volume payment and UPI transaction flows using Apache JMeter, validating system behaviour under 2x–3x peak load conditions.
Supported successful release sign-offs by ensuring end-to-end test coverage across UI automation, API testing, backend validation, and integration testing, reducing post-release defects and rework.

projects.title: Payment Gateway Automation
projects.summary: Automated UI and API test cases for onboarding and transaction flows using Selenium, Cucumber, and REST Assured, ensuring end-to-end validation.

caseStudy.overview: Worked as a Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET) on a large-scale enterprise Payment Services Platform responsible for managing the complete lifecycle of digital payment transactions.
caseStudy.businessDomain: Payment Gateway, Merchant Acquiring, Clearing & Settlement, Reconciliations
caseStudy.businessProblem: Financial payment platforms must process millions of secure transactions with near-zero failure rates. Any issue in authorization, routing, clearing, or settlement can directly impact merchants and banks.
caseStudy.businessContext: The system processes high-volume Card and UPI transactions, requiring an automated validation approach that guarantees zero defects in production.
responsibilities: Designed the end-to-end regression automation framework from scratch.
Automated critical merchant checkout and onboarding workflows.
Validated ISO-8583 transaction messaging across the acquiring switch.
Built data-driven API suites for complex payload validations.
caseStudy.testingStrategy: A decoupled, risk-based approach prioritizing API contracts first, followed by UI workflows, and finally performance testing.
caseStudy.framework: Data-Driven Page Object Model using Java, Cucumber, and TestNG.
caseStudy.uiAutomation: Implemented highly scalable Selenium scripts handling dynamic iframes (payment pages) and multi-tab workflows for merchant onboarding.
caseStudy.apiAutomation: Developed REST Assured suites utilizing POJOs and Jackson for robust payload serialization and deserialization.
caseStudy.dbValidation: Wrote complex JDBC wrappers to cross-verify MongoDB transaction logs against MySQL settlement ledgers.
caseStudy.performanceTesting: Injected loads of 5,000+ TPS using distributed Apache JMeter to locate database locking and memory leak bottlenecks.
caseStudy.cicd: Automated regression execution via Jenkins nightly builds with custom ExtentReports.
challenges: Dynamic Webhooks: Overcame asynchronous webhook validation by polling database states.
Stale Elements in React UI: Implemented custom fluent waits resolving 99% of synchronization flakiness.
caseStudy.impact: Drastically reduced post-release hotfixes and automated 95% of the manual regression cycle.
achievements: Identified memory leaks at 3x peak load.
Automated 2,000+ test scenarios across UI and API.
testingMetrics: Execution time reduced from 2 days to 3 hours
caseStudy.lessonsLearned: API-first testing significantly reduces flakiness compared to UI-heavy validation in Fintech applications.

projects.title: API Automation Framework
projects.summary: Developed reusable REST Assured components and maintained Postman collections with Newman scheduler for continuous API regression testing.

caseStudy.overview: A reusable API testing framework validating backend endpoints dynamically.
caseStudy.businessProblem: Manual API regression testing creates development bottlenecks during sprint releases.
caseStudy.businessContext: Designed a dynamic, reusable API suite scheduler validating transaction payloads under tight SLAs.
responsibilities: Built a reusable Java-based REST Assured wrapper.
caseStudy.testingStrategy: API-first regression suite execution in CI/CD pipeline.
challenges: Payload complexity resolved by creating custom JSON parsers.
caseStudy.impact: Ensured continuous build integration with automated test reports.
achievements: Reduced API regression cycle from 4 hours to 10 minutes.
caseStudy.lessonsLearned: Standardized schema checks catch downstream integration bugs early.

projects.title: Performance Testing Suite
projects.summary: Designed and executed Apache JMeter test plans and ran master–slave distributed tests to analyze throughput, latency, and error rates under peak load.

caseStudy.overview: A distributed load injection framework testing transaction switches under production peak volume simulation.
caseStudy.businessProblem: Switches experience connection timeouts during high transaction spikes.
caseStudy.businessContext: Load injection validated transaction switch latency thresholds.
responsibilities: Designed distributed JMeter master-slave thread groups.
challenges: Database locks resolved via connection pool tuning.
caseStudy.impact: Prevented production timeouts during payment cycles.
caseStudy.lessonsLearned: Distributed slaves are required to avoid load generator resource exhaustion.

projects.title: Backend Validation & Monitoring
projects.summary: Performed SQL and MongoDB validation and analyzed Grafana dashboards for system stability and SLA monitoring.

caseStudy.overview: Data reconciliation checks verifying payment ledgers across database platforms post checkout flows.
caseStudy.businessProblem: Discrepancies in ledger records post checkout failures.
caseStudy.businessContext: Synchronous database queries executing assertions post-run.
responsibilities: Wrote direct SQL joins verifying transaction records.
caseStudy.testingStrategy: Data verification pipeline post transactional execution.
challenges: Handling nested MongoDB structures using automated parsers.
caseStudy.lessonsLearned: Automated data reconciliation catches asynchronous write issues.

education.institution: Teegala Krishna Reddy Engineering College, Hyderabad
